player_notes.brandon ingram: Ruled OUT for Game 6 vs. Cavaliers with right heel inflammation. His absence boosts usage for RJ Barrett and other Raptors — note: Game 6 already played, monitor status for Game 7.
player_notes.luka doncic: Per Windhorst, Luka is 'not close' to returning. Remains OUT as Lakers advance to face Thunder in Round 2.
player_notes.paul george: Resurgent 23-point performance in Game 6 win; appears healthy and back in form heading into Game 7 vs. Celtics.

game_notes.TOR vs CLE: Brandon Ingram was OUT for Game 6; his availability for Game 7 is uncertain — if still out, Barrett and other Raptors see elevated role and usage.
game_notes.LAL vs OKC: Luka Doncic confirmed 'not close' to returning, so Lakers will face Thunder without him. LeBron James logged 28 points in Game 6 closeout, remains primary offensive engine.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Game 6 (May 1) — DET 93, ORL 79 | DET wins series 4-2 — SERIES CLINCHED (margin 14)
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news reports 'Pistons rally from 24 down to beat Magic 93-79 and force Game 7' — the series is now tied 3-3 with a Game 7 to be played, not a DET series win. The final score (93-79) matches but the series outcome in the context doc is wrong: it says DET won 4-2, but news confirms this was G6 and DET forced Game 7.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update DET vs ORL series record to 3-3. Remove 'SERIES CLINCHED' designation. Add G6 diary entry reflecting the 24-point comeback and upcoming Game 7. The context doc incorrectly recorded G6 as a series clincher for DET when in fact ORL led 3-1 going into G5, DET won G5 and G6 to force G7.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Game 6 (May 1) — TOR 112, CLE 110 | Series tied 3-3 — TOR FORCES G7 (margin 2, OT)
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news confirms 'Barrett hits winning 3-pointer in OT as Raptors force Game 7, beat Cavaliers 112-110' — this is consistent with the context doc's G6 result. However, the news also references 'All-Star forward Brandon Ingram out with heel inflammation' being ruled out for Game 6, and the context doc's G6 diary already reflects Ingram's absence. No direct conflict on the series result, but the news confirms three Game 7s are upcoming (BOS-PHI, DET-ORL, CLE-TOR), which aligns with context.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: No change needed for series status. Confirm Ingram's heel inflammation status for Game 7 — news headline 'All-Star forward Brandon Ingram out with heel inflammation' references G6 ruling, but G7 availability remains uncertain and should be flagged as the defining TOR variable.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Game 6 (May 1) — LAL 98, HOU 78 | LAL wins series 4-2 — SERIES CLINCHED (margin 20)
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News confirms 'Lakers close out Rockets in Game 6 rout' with LeBron scoring 28 points in a 98-78 win — consistent with context doc. No conflict on series result. However, the news headline 'Windy: Luka is not close to returning' directly updates the context doc's note that Luka's 'Realistic: R2 (~May 3-4)' — Windhorst is now reporting Luka is NOT close to returning for R2 against OKC.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update Luka Doncic injury entry: Remove 'Realistic: R2 (~May 3-4)' timeline. Per Windhorst (May 2), Luka 'is not close' to returning for the LAL-OKC series. LAL's R2 offensive architecture remains LeBron-dependent with Reaves as secondary option. The R2 context note about Luka's AST dossier update remains valid but his unavailability for early R2 games should be flagged as near-certain.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: The context doc's DEN vs MIN G6 diary entry describes McDaniels 32/10/3 leading MIN, with Randle 18/4/5, Gobert 10/13/8. No mention of Terrence Shannon Jr. starting.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News headline states 'Jaden McDaniels leads Timberwolves on both ends of the court in 110-98 clincher to oust Nuggets in 6' and the description mentions 'Terrence Shannon Jr. scored 24 points in a surprise start for the injury-ravaged Minnesota' — Shannon Jr. started and scored 24 points, which is not reflected in the context doc's G6 diary.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update MIN G6 diary to include Terrence Shannon Jr.'s 24-point surprise start. This confirms MIN's injury depth (Edwards OUT) forced a roster reshuffling beyond what was documented. Note Shannon Jr. as a potential R2 contributor if Edwards remains out.
